test.name: Source-vs-source disagreement should route to conflict-resolution
test.description: User describes two sources disagreeing on a fact for the same person (Ireland vs. County Cork). That's a conflict between sources, not a single-person impossibility, so it belongs to conflict-resolution. Tests the boundary between check-warnings (single-person impossibilities) and conflict-resolution (multi-source disagreements). The brief explicitly names this boundary.

input.user_message: One census says he was born in Ireland, the death cert says County Cork -- flag that mismatch.

negative.explanation: A source-vs-source disagreement is a conflict, not a warning. Warnings are about a single person's data violating physical/biological/temporal constraints; conflicts are about two sources giving different answers for the same fact. The skills' references/warnings-as-identity-signals.md draws this boundary explicitly: 'Conflicts: two or more sources disagree about the same fact for the same person... handled by conflict-resolution.'

judge_context: Should NOT invoke person_warnings -- the user isn't asking about a single-person impossibility
Should route to conflict-resolution because the prompt describes two sources disagreeing on the same fact
If the skill is invoked anyway, it should hand off to conflict-resolution without running the warning checks
